summary: permcserver ships native VLESS/WebSocket proxy backdoor with hardcoded attacker UUID
details: The package ships a Linux shared library (lib-linux.so) that implements a VLESS-over-WebSocket proxy server (V2Ray/Xray protocol) with a hardcoded 32-character authentication UUID (4916e0f5-8abc-4ad4-ba5d-158582c60d85). The binary exports `vserver_start`/`vserver_stop` symbols intended to be invoked through the declared `koffi` FFI dependency. On startup, it probes environment variables commonly set by game-hosting control panels (PTERODACTYL_PORT, MINECRAFT_PORT, MC_PORT, GAME_PORT, SERVER_PORT, APP_PORT, ERGODUANKOU) to choose its bind port, then serves a fake HTML page titled ':: SYSTEM ONLINE:: MINECRAFT SERVER ONLINE' to disguise the listener as a legitimate game server. Any client holding the hardcoded UUID can perform WebSocket upgrade + CONNECT tunneling through the installer's host, turning the machine into an attacker-controlled TCP proxy relay using the installer's IP address and bandwidth. The package name and cover HTML impersonate a Pterodactyl/Minecraft helper to blend into game-hosting infrastructure. The declared `main: index.js` is absent from the tarball; the.so is the actual shipped payload, loadable via the bundled koffi FFI binding.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
